title: Soil Analysis Comparison Dashboard
description: Compare a questioned soil sample (from a suspect's boots) against three reference samples across four criteria at once: Munsell color, particle-size distribution (gravel/sand/silt/clay), pH, and density-gradient banding. Click a reference column to compare it with the questioned sample, press Match Score to compute a weighted similarity score, and New Case to load a fresh scenario. Matching gradient bands are outlined green and non-matching bands red.
creator: Forensic Science Intelligent Textbook
subject: Forensic Science
Trace Evidence
Soil Analysis
Geology
Physical Evidence Comparison
bloomLevel: Analyze (L4) — compare a questioned soil sample to reference samples across multiple properties simultaneously.
chapter_title: Glass, Soil, and Physical Trace Evidence

educational.topic: Comparing a questioned soil sample to known references using color, particle size, pH, and density-gradient banding
learningObjectives: Compare a questioned soil sample to reference samples across four independent criteria at once.
Read specific numeric values for pH and gravel/sand/silt/clay percentages and judge how closely two samples agree.
Interpret a density-gradient column: recognize which mineral bands align between samples and which do not.
Synthesize the four lines of evidence into an overall match judgment and recognize that a single matching property is weak while agreement across all four is strong.
educational.prerequisites: Awareness that soil varies from place to place and that trace evidence can link a person to a location

pedagogical.interactionPattern: A 2x2 dashboard shows the same four samples (Questioned, Reference A, B, C) as columns within each of four criterion panels: Munsell color swatches, a stacked particle-size bar chart, a pH scale with markers, and a density-gradient tube with colored bands. Clicking any reference column selects it for comparison and highlights it (and the questioned column) across all four panels. A 'Match Score' button computes a weighted similarity (color distance, pH difference, particle-size overlap, band-match fraction) and prints a percentage. In the gradient-tube panel the selected reference's bands are outlined green where they align with a questioned band and red where they do not. Hovering any panel shows a tooltip explaining that criterion's forensic significance. 'New Case' loads a different scenario.
pedagogical.assessment: Analysis through simultaneous comparison: the learner must inspect four criteria, decide which reference best matches the questioned sample, and confirm with the computed match score. The default scenario is designed so Reference C (exact location) scores high, Reference B (nearby) is intermediate, and Reference A (distant, different geology) scores low, letting students check whether their visual judgment matches the quantitative score.
pedagogical.modelType: Comparison/scoring model: each sample is a record of Munsell notation, an RGB color, four particle-size percentages, a pH value, and a list of density-gradient bands (height + color). Similarity is computed per criterion and averaged into a 0-100 match score; band matching uses a height-and-color tolerance test against the questioned sample.
controls: Clickable reference columns (A, B, C) to select the comparison sample
'Match Score' button — compute and display the weighted similarity score
'New Case' button — load a different questioned/reference scenario
Hover tooltips on each of the four criterion panels
